title: tragacantha fendleri
intro: Tragacantha fendleri, also known as Fendler's milkvetch, is a perennial legume native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and relieving pain, and may also have antibacterial and antifungal properties. Fendler's milkvetch can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is sometimes used in herbal remedies to treat wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues.
